items.question: Is my donation tax-deductible?
items.answer: Yes. Sunshine on a Ranney Day is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit (EIN 45-4773997). You'll receive a tax receipt from Zeffy immediately after donating.

items.question: Does 100% of my donation really go to kids?
items.answer: Yes. Zeffy is a 100% free platform for nonprofits — they charge zero processing fees. Your entire donation goes directly to funding room materials, labor, and design for children's spaces.

items.question: Can I set up a monthly recurring donation?
items.answer: Absolutely. In the donation form above, simply toggle to "Monthly" before selecting your amount. Monthly gifts help us plan future room makeovers with greater confidence.

items.question: Can I donate materials or services instead?
items.answer: Yes! We partner with contractors, designers, and suppliers across Atlanta. Visit our Get Involved page to learn about partnership opportunities.

items.question: How much does a typical room makeover cost?
items.answer: Room costs vary based on scope — a dream bedroom averages $8,000–$12,000, while an accessible bathroom can run $10,000–$15,000. Every dollar donated goes toward these transformations.

items.question: What is Sunshine on a Ranney Day?
items.answer: Sunshine on a Ranney Day (SOARD) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that creates life-changing home makeovers — dream bedrooms, accessible bathrooms, and therapy rooms — for children with special needs in the greater Atlanta area, all at no cost to families.

items.question: How long has SOARD been operating?
items.answer: We've been building rooms and changing lives since 2012. To date, we've completed over 106 rooms for more than 132 children across metro Atlanta.

items.question: Who runs the organization?
items.answer: SOARD was co-founded by Peter and Kristen Ranney. Our team includes dedicated volunteers, board members, and a network of construction and design partners. Visit our Leadership page to meet the team.

items.question: What types of rooms do you build?
items.answer: We build three types of spaces: dream bedrooms designed around a child's interests, accessible bathrooms that promote independence, and therapy rooms for at-home physical or occupational therapy.

items.question: How long does a room makeover take?
items.answer: Most rooms are completed in 1–3 days during a scheduled build weekend. The planning and design phase typically takes 4–6 weeks before the build.

items.question: Who actually builds the rooms?
items.answer: Our rooms are built by a combination of licensed contractors, professional designers, and skilled volunteers. Every project is managed to ensure safety and quality.

items.question: How do families apply for a room makeover?
items.answer: Families can apply through our Application page. We accept applications year-round and select children based on need and available resources.

items.question: Who qualifies for a room makeover?
items.answer: We serve children with special needs (physical, developmental, or medical) in the greater Atlanta area. Families must demonstrate financial need and own or have permission to modify their home.

items.question: Is there any cost to the family?
items.answer: None. Every room makeover is provided completely free of charge to the family. All materials, labor, and design are funded through donations and partnerships.
